name: Very Buttery Challah
recipeInstructions:
1. Dissolve yeast in warm water.
2. Beat 3 eggs in a large mixing bowl; add salt, sugar, dissolved yeast, and butter. Mix well.
3. Gradually add 4 cups of flour to the mixture, stirring continuously until incorporated.
4. Add the remaining 3 cups of flour, one at a time, stirring after each addition, until the dough is soft and workable.
5. Knead the dough on a floured surface until it is no longer sticky and feels smooth when touched.
6. Place the dough in a greased bowl, cover with a towel, and let rise for 1 1/2 hours, or until doubled in size.
7. Punch down the dough, knead briefly, and divide into six equal pieces.
8. Roll each piece by hand into long, thin ropes. Braid three ropes together, pinching the ends to secure. Repeat with the remaining three ropes.
9. Place each braided loaf on a greased baking sheet, cover with a towel, and let rise for about an hour until doubled in size.
10. Brush the loaves with the 2 beaten eggs, sprinkle with poppy seeds or sesame seeds if desired.
11. Bake in a preheated oven at 350°F (175°C) for about 45 minutes, or until golden-brown.
